24.reason: [Using `gets()` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers. Use `fgets()` instead.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Using `gets()` is unsafe and deprecated; it was removed in C11 and causes compilation errors with modern compilers. Use `fgets()` instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and deprecated; it may cause buffer overflow. More critically, in many modern environments (like those with GCC >= 7), `gets` is removed entirely, leading to a compilation error: "implicit declaration of function 鈥榞ets鈥" or "鈥榞ets鈥 was not declared". This explains the observed compilation error "gcc: not found" 鈥 actually, the real issue is that `gets` is undefined. Use `fgets` instead.]

30.reason: [This `continue` skips incrementing `i`, causing an infinite loop when encountering a space.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [This `continue` skips incrementing `i`, causing an infinite loop when encountering a space.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This `continue` causes an infinite loop because `i` is never incremented when encountering a space. The loop condition depends on `s[i] != '='`, but if there's a space, `i` stays the same, so it keeps checking the same space forever.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This `continue` causes an infinite loop because `i` is not incremented when encountering a space.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [This `continue` skips the rest of the loop body but does not increment `i`, causing an infinite loop when a space is encountered.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [This `continue` causes an infinite loop because `i` is not incremented when encountering a space. The loop condition depends on `i`, but `i++` is skipped.]

74.reason: [This line assumes all operators are '+' or '-', but after the first pass, some operators were replaced with arbitrary values like `44 - q`, which are not valid operators, leading to incorrect behavior or undefined results.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This line assumes all operators are either '+' or '-', but after the first pass, some operators (like '*' and '/') were replaced with arbitrary values (e.g., `44 - q`). Casting these back to `char` and passing to `f` leads to incorrect behavior or undefined operations. Also, this second pass doesn't skip already-processed multiplication/division tokens, causing invalid calculations.]

77.reason: [The indexing `num[2*k]` assumes the result is stored at that position, but due to flawed parsing and overwriting logic (especially with `num[p-1]=0` and arbitrary operator replacement), the final result may not be at `num[2*k]`. This leads to wrong output even if the program compiles and runs.]

57.reason: [Arbitrary assignment to `num[p]` (e.g., 44 - q) corrupts operator data. This breaks later parsing since the operator is no longer '+', '-', etc., causing incorrect behavior in the second pass.]
